message:
fix(collect): use non-blocking drain to prevent grandchild hang

When a process exits but leaves grandchild processes holding the pipe
open, drain_to_limit would block indefinitely waiting for EOF. This
caused the tool_runner_hang test to fail with a 5+ second timeout.

Fix: Use non-blocking reads in drain_to_limit on Unix. After the direct
child exits, we only want to collect data immediately available in the
pipe buffer, not wait for grandchildren that may never exit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
